name: Charles Earland
abstract: Charles Earland (May 24, 1941 – December 11, 1999) was an American jazz organist and pianist.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Jazz on the Hammond
One Night Stand: A Keyboard Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Eric Alexander
about_quotes.evidence: I remember playing with Charles Earland in this huge room in Rochester, it was Thanksgiving 1990.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eric Alexander interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, we played a lot of things together. ... We were on that CTI tour. ... We’ve done recordings with Quincy, of course, and some other things we’ve done together
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: He learned how to play the blues by being with Charles Earland, an association.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010
